title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
notes: Use for cybersecurity risk-management framing, Govern/Identify/Protect/Detect/Respond/Recover language, and general knowledge questions.

title: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
notes: Primary source for incident response evaluation and expected response ordering.

title: MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise Matrix
notes: Use for high-level tactics and technique families. Prefer STIX data for machine-readable mapping.

title: MITRE ATT&CK Version History
notes: Record the ATT&CK version used so the paper can report source freshness.

title: MITRE ATT&CK STIX Data
notes: Use for machine-readable ATT&CK technique IDs, names, descriptions, and tactic mappings.

title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
notes: Primary source for prompt injection, excessive agency, overreliance, and LLM application risks.

title: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
benchmark_categories: Hallucination Resistance; Cybersecurity Knowledge; Incident Response
notes: Use for questions about known exploitation status. Must record download/access date.

title: NVD Vulnerability APIs
notes: Use to verify whether CVEs exist and to prevent fake-CVE hallucinations.

title: CWE Top 25 Most Dangerous Software Weaknesses
notes: Use for weakness knowledge such as SQL injection, improper input validation, and file upload weaknesses.

title: CWE Top 25 2024 Methodology
notes: Use for explaining how CWE Top 25 relates to CVE records and weakness prevalence.

title: CISA Cybersecurity Alerts and Advisories
benchmark_categories: Cybersecurity Knowledge; Incident Response; Alert Classification
notes: Use selectively. Do not overload RAG with too many advisories; choose relevant recent examples.

title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
notes: Use for AI risk-management framing, not for core cybersecurity answers.

title: OWASP API Security Top 10
notes: Useful for API abuse and access-control scenarios if those appear in the benchmark.

title: OWASP Web Security Testing Guide
notes: Useful for general web security concepts and defensive testing terminology.

title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 Security and Privacy Controls
notes: Useful if the paper needs control language, but not necessary for first RAG build.

title: CISA StopRansomware Guidance
notes: Useful for ransomware response and prevention scenarios.

title: Microsoft Security Incident Response Guide
notes: Useful as practical vendor guidance, but should not outrank NIST in paper framing.

title: Google Cloud Security Incident Response Guidance
notes: Optional practical source for cloud incident response.
